title: Numbered DOE row enumeration priority
problem_class: Stage2 under-enumeration for explicit numbered DOE or design-table formulations
description: When Stage1 table assets preserve explicit numbered DOE rows, deterministic table-derived rows should enter the active Stage2 candidate surface and take priority over overlapping generic numeric LLM rows.
expected_intervention_points: Stage1 table assets expose an explicit numbered DOE or design table.
Stage2 deterministically enumerates numbered rows.
Stage2 merge prefers structured numbered DOE rows over overlapping llm_extracted numeric rows.
activation_signals: weak_labels__v7pilot_r3_fixparse.tsv contains candidate_source=doe_numbered_table_row or candidate_source=doe_numbered_table_row_recovery for a paper with a known numbered DOE table
numbered_doe_row_candidates_summary_v1.tsv reports numbered_rows_found for the same run
Overlapping numeric llm_extracted rows are absent or reduced after the Stage2 merge
notes: UFXX9WXE is the motivating benchmark case. The feature existed in later same-day child validation/support lineages but the benchmark-valid parent run still reused an older Stage2 artifact with only five UFXX rows. The reporting contract now treats the explicit deterministic recovery label as a governed synonym for activation credit.

title: Numbered DOE regression guard
problem_class: Silent shrinkage between deterministic numbered table rows and the active Stage2 candidate surface
description: Per-paper deterministic guard emitted by the active Stage2 extractor to record whether explicit numbered DOE table rows are actually preserved in the run's merged Stage2 candidate surface.
expected_intervention_points: Active Stage2 run writes numbered_doe_regression_guard_v1.tsv
Guard compares numbered_table_row_count to the formulation-like Stage2 candidate count
Guard flags fail or warn when the active Stage2 candidate surface is smaller than the explicit numbered DOE table surface or still depends on overlapping numeric LLM rows
activation_signals: numbered_doe_regression_guard_v1.tsv exists in the run output
pilot_summary.json records numbered_doe_guard_tsv
Guard rows contain deterministic statuses pass, warn, or fail
notes: This guard exists specifically to distinguish code presence from run activation after the UFXX9WXE lineage-reuse failure.

title: Table-first evidence binding
problem_class: Loss of structured table-row anchors when evidence is taken only from broad full-text windows
description: When structured table rows are available, table-derived formulation candidates should carry explicit table-row evidence anchors rather than relying only on broad full-text windows.
expected_intervention_points: Stage1 table assets preserve usable row structure
Stage2 candidate rows carry table_row evidence with an explicit table identifier
Downstream final outputs retain those evidence anchors when rows survive closure
activation_signals: weak_labels__v7pilot_r3_fixparse.tsv contains instance_evidence_region_type=table_row
evidence_section records a numbered table identifier such as <paper_key>__numbered_doe_table_01
final_formulation_table_v1.tsv retains table-row evidence for surviving rows
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on table_row anchors
notes: Current activation reporting can detect this feature only when run artifacts expose explicit table_row evidence anchors. Governed numbered DOE recovery rows are accepted when they carry the same table-row anchor structure.

title: Stage 2 ordered input evidence packing
problem_class: Stage2 live prompt assembly can over-weight raw-prefix text when a governed ordered evidence pack would ground the model better
description: Governed Stage2 live prompt assembly may switch from raw-prefix text to an ordered evidence pack that prioritizes synthesis/preparation, materials/procurement, table evidence, and narrative fallback for stronger semantic grounding through the canonical S2-2 evidence artifact contract.
expected_intervention_points: Stage2 live execution persists evidence_blocks_v1.json before prompt assembly.
Prompt assembly reads the resolved evidence artifact instead of silently recomputing pre-LLM evidence selection from clean text.
The prompt preview records the prompt layout class, the packing mode, the ordered block sequence, and the canonical evidence artifact path.
activation_signals: evidence_blocks_v1.json exists under semantic_stage2_objects/evidence_blocks/<paper_key>/ for the run-local Stage2 output
stage2_prompt_preview_v1.tsv records input_packing_mode=ordered_blocks, prompt_layout_class=ordered_controlled_evidence_pack, and an evidence_artifact_path
The run-local feature activation report marks the feature active only when the controlled ordering mode is actually used from the persisted evidence artifact
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on ordered Stage2 input packing output
notes: This is an execution feature unit with run-level observability. It records whether the governed ordered evidence pack was actually used through the canonical persisted S2-2 evidence artifact and the derived prompt-preview surface.

title: S2 candidate section-aware split
problem_class: Role-aware selection becomes unstable when maintained Stage2 candidate generation leaves recoverable structural regions hidden inside coarse prose blocks
description: The maintained Stage2 pre-LLM path now persists candidate_blocks_v1.json as an explicit candidate-segmentation surface and records section-aware prose candidates before role-aware evidence selection begins.
expected_intervention_points: extract_semantic_stage2_objects_v2.py persists candidate_blocks_v1.json before evidence_blocks_v1.json is resolved.
Prose candidates record a segmentation_method plus section_kind metadata so the boundary is inspectable before selector prioritization.
Run-local candidate segmentation debug output can expose the same pre-selector candidate surface.
activation_signals: candidate_blocks_v1.json exists under semantic_stage2_objects/candidate_blocks/<paper_key>/ for the run-local Stage2 output
The artifact records segmentation_profile=section_aware_candidate_segmentation_v1
At least one prose candidate records segmentation_method and section_kind fields
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on explicit candidate segmentation
notes: This feature makes candidate segmentation explicit and inspectable without creating a parallel Stage2 route.

title: S2 candidate table isolation
problem_class: Table-derived selector candidates become unreliable when adjacent body text, page furniture, or sparse fragments leak into the maintained pre-selector table surface
description: The maintained candidate-segmentation layer isolates table candidates before role-aware selection and records them in candidate_blocks_v1.json so table quality can be inspected independently of selector scoring.
expected_intervention_points: The candidate artifact records table candidates separately from prose candidates.
Table candidates preserve caption or title context where available and carry quality flags when rows were filtered or the table looks sparse or corrupted.
The maintained table-selection debug surface remains downstream observability rather than the primary candidate source.
activation_signals: candidate_blocks_v1.json contains candidate_type=table rows
feature_activation_snapshot.table_isolation is true for artifacts with table assets
Candidate table rows record table quality metadata before selector role assignment
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on candidate table isolation
notes: This feature governs the pre-selector table surface only; it does not change downstream Stage3 semantics.

title: S2 candidate noise filtering
problem_class: Selector quality degrades when obvious publisher furniture, download text, or repeated non-content rows enter the maintained candidate surface as if they were real evidence
description: The maintained candidate-segmentation layer applies conservative high-confidence noise filtering before selector prioritization and records the result in candidate_blocks_v1.json and candidate_segmentation_debug_v1.tsv.
expected_intervention_points: Paragraph candidate generation removes obvious publisher, download, or page-furniture lines.
Table candidate generation filters obvious non-content rows before summary rendering.
Candidate-level observability records noise or quality flags without collapsing them into selector role semantics.
activation_signals: candidate_blocks_v1.json records feature_activation_snapshot.noise_filtering=true
candidate_segmentation_debug_v1.tsv exists in the run-local analysis directory
Candidate entries may expose noise_flags or quality_flags before selector prioritization
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on candidate noise filtering
notes: This feature is conservative by design and exists to make candidate-level contamination inspectable in maintained runs.

title: S2-2 evidence artifact contract
problem_class: Pre-LLM evidence selection and ordering can remain implicit, in-memory, or bypassable when the maintained Stage2 path does not persist a canonical governed evidence artifact
description: The maintained Stage2 path persists evidence_blocks_v1.json as the canonical S2-2 clean-text-to-governed-evidence-package boundary before any live prompt assembly or replay normalization proceeds.
expected_intervention_points: extract_semantic_stage2_objects_v2.py resolves pre-LLM evidence blocks before the LLM call or replay normalization path.
The maintained Stage2 output stores one evidence_blocks_v1.json per paper under semantic_stage2_objects/evidence_blocks/<paper_key>/.
Prompt preview and downstream audit surfaces point back to the canonical evidence artifact rather than acting as the primary source of truth.
activation_signals: evidence_blocks_v1.json exists in the run-local Stage2 semantic output
The artifact records paper_key, source_clean_text_path, source_manifest_path, input_contract, evidence_blocks, coverage_summary, feature_activation_snapshot, technical_status, and design_status
The run-local feature activation report can validate the artifact contract from the run outputs directly
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on the S2-2 artifact contract
notes: This feature makes the pre-LLM evidence package a maintained execution boundary rather than a transient debug view.

title: S2-2 technical versus design success split
problem_class: A Stage2 run can emit an evidence artifact while still silently violating the intended pre-LLM input contract unless technical completeness and design conformance are separated
description: The canonical S2-2 evidence artifact records technical_status separately from design_status so artifact generation is not mistaken for input-contract success.
expected_intervention_points: evidence_blocks_v1.json records technical_status for artifact readability and required-field completeness.
evidence_blocks_v1.json records design_status for input-contract satisfaction and required-feature activation.
Prompt preview and run-level observability surfaces can expose the split without collapsing it into one success field.
activation_signals: The evidence artifact contains both technical_status and design_status objects
technical_status records required-field completeness and overall status
design_status records input_contract_satisfied, required_features_active, and overall
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on the S2-2 status split
notes: This feature prevents a generated artifact from being treated as normal design success when the intended S2-2 contract path was not actually satisfied.

title: S2-2 prompt preview derived from evidence artifact
problem_class: Prompt-preview observability can drift away from the real pre-LLM evidence body when the preview is generated independently of the maintained evidence artifact
description: The maintained Stage2 prompt preview is a derived observability surface only. It must point to and summarize the same evidence_blocks_v1.json artifact used for prompt assembly.
expected_intervention_points: Prompt preview rows include evidence_artifact_path plus summarized technical and design statuses.
Prompt assembly uses the canonical evidence artifact so the preview is generated from the same resolved evidence body.
Run-level activation reporting can validate that the preview rows resolve back to existing evidence artifacts.
activation_signals: stage2_prompt_preview_v1.tsv contains evidence_artifact_path, technical_status_overall, and design_status_overall
The referenced evidence artifact exists for each prompt-preview row
The summarized status fields match the canonical artifact
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on derived preview linkage
notes: This feature does not create a second evidence surface. It ensures the prompt-preview artifact remains derived observability only.

title: S2-2 role-aware evidence selection
problem_class: Pure global block ranking can crowd out required pre-LLM evidence roles, leaving the maintained Stage2 evidence artifact technically complete but poorly structured for formulation discovery
description: The maintained S2-2 selector deterministically assigns candidate evidence into explicit roles, scores within role, and persists the chosen role assignments plus weak or missing roles in evidence_blocks_v1.json.
expected_intervention_points: extract_semantic_stage2_objects_v2.py uses a deterministic role-aware selector profile before prompt assembly.
evidence_blocks_v1.json records selector_profile, required_roles, selected_roles, missing_or_weak_roles, and per-block role_assignment fields.
The maintained prompt preview remains derived from the same evidence artifact rather than running an independent selector.
activation_signals: The evidence artifact contains selector_profile
Evidence blocks record role_assignment, role_priority, and role_score_breakdown
The evidence artifact records required_roles, selected_roles, and missing_or_weak_roles
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on S2-2 role-aware selection
notes: This feature upgrades the maintained S2-2 selector quality without introducing a second LLM or a parallel Stage2 route.

title: S2-2 DOE overlay selection
problem_class: DOE or optimization papers need different evidence-role priorities than general formulation papers, but the maintained selector can miss design and variable surfaces if it does not activate a governed overlay
description: When deterministic pre-LLM signals indicate a DOE or optimization paper, the maintained S2-2 selector activates a DOE overlay that adds experimental-design and variable-table priorities on top of the general selector profile.
expected_intervention_points: DOE-like papers are detected from maintained structural and lexical signals only.
evidence_blocks_v1.json records archetype_overlay=doe_optimization_v1 when the overlay is active.
The role summary for a DOE paper can expose weak or missing design-related roles directly in the canonical artifact.
activation_signals: feature_activation_snapshot.doe_pre_llm_detection is true for a paper
The evidence artifact records archetype_overlay=doe_optimization_v1
DOE overlay roles appear in required_roles for the affected evidence artifact
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on DOE overlay activation
notes: This is an overlay on the maintained selector, not a separate pipeline or a second LLM call.

title: S2-2 duplicate table suppression
problem_class: Near-duplicate extracted tables can consume the limited S2-2 evidence budget and crowd out more informative roles when the maintained selector treats them as independent wins
description: The maintained S2-2 selector suppresses duplicate tables using deterministic shape, row-label preview, and sampled-content signatures, and records any suppression events in the canonical evidence artifact.
expected_intervention_points: Role-aware table selection checks duplicate signatures before final inclusion.
Suppressed duplicates are recorded in duplicate_table_suppression_events inside evidence_blocks_v1.json.
Table-selection debug remains derived observability and can show which tables were selected versus suppressed.
activation_signals: The evidence artifact contains duplicate_table_suppression_events
At least one evidence artifact in a run records a duplicate suppression event when duplicate tables are present
The feature activation report can distinguish runs with no duplicates from runs where suppression actually fired
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on duplicate-table suppression
notes: This feature records when duplicate suppression actually fired; it should not claim activation just because the code path exists.

title: Stage2 JSON sanitation Path 1
problem_class: Malformed or truncated raw LLM JSON at the Stage2 parse boundary
scope: Post-raw-output parse repair inside the current Stage2 boundary; frozen raw baseline diagnostics; no downstream stage mutation
forbidden_behavior: No semantic reconstruction, no inferred fields, no tolerant fallback parser, no downstream identity reinterpretation
output_artifact_contract: Produces per-paper parsed JSON artifacts plus path1_parse_audit.tsv, path1_failure_subset.tsv, and path1_vs_path2_comparison.tsv; preserves the original raw response artifact unchanged
description: Conservative syntax-first sanitation before strict parse. Applies low-risk mechanical cleanup only: code-fence trimming, leading-junk trimming before the first JSON start, and narrow balanced-close repair when the document is clearly truncated at the tail.
expected_intervention_points: A raw Stage2 response is already saved and is passed through sanitize_stage2_json_text before strict json.loads
The sanitizer can remove formatting wrappers or perform narrow balanced-close repair without inventing content
The diagnostic comparison artifacts prove whether Path 1 modified text or parsed directly
activation_signals: path1_parse_audit.tsv exists for the diagnostic baseline
path1_parse_audit.tsv records parse_stage values of direct, sanitized, or balanced_close
path1_vs_path2_comparison.tsv records Path 1 as the preferred path when Path 2 is unnecessary or riskier
notes: This is the current conservative parse-repair path. It is syntax-first and auditable, and it is suitable for managed governance because it does not reconstruct semantics.

title: Stage2 legacy fixparse fallback Path 2
problem_class: Historical broad fallback parse repair for raw Stage2 responses that fail the current conservative sanitizer
scope: Historical fixparse comparison only; Path 1 failures on frozen raw baselines; diagnostic evaluation without promotion to mainline authority
forbidden_behavior: No use as active mainline Stage2 authority, no silent semantic reconstruction, no hidden replacement of the current conservative sanitizer
output_artifact_contract: Produces path2_parse_audit.tsv only for Path 1 failures; any rescued paper must be traceable to the original raw response and the repair stage used
description: Historical fixparse basis derived from the legacy wide-row extractor. It strips code fences, then attempts a broad brace-window recovery before falling back to a default empty schema object. This is diagnostic-only and broader than syntax-only repair.
expected_intervention_points: Path 1 has already failed on a frozen raw response
The historical fixparse fallback is used only for diagnostic comparison, not mainline processing
A Path 2 audit row records the exact repair stage and any risk classification
activation_signals: path2_parse_audit.tsv exists and has rows for a Path 1 failure subset
path1_vs_path2_comparison.tsv records a Path 2 attempt only for Path 1 failures
The diagnostic summary records whether any rescue required possible_semantic_rewrite or mixed_or_unclear classification
notes: This is a diagnostic-only fallback basis. It remains historical context unless future evidence proves it can be governed without semantic-riskful repair.

title: Variant-aware GT authority switch
problem_class: GT authority drift when benchmark comparison continues to use an older workbook after the benchmark object changes
description: The DEV15 benchmark compare node should explicitly point to the versioned variant-aware GT workbook and record that authority in run outputs.
expected_intervention_points: compare_final_table_to_gt_v1.py reads the variant-aware GT workbook
benchmark outputs preserve the authority path in counts-by-doi artifacts
GT-authority-specific subdirectories or outputs distinguish the newer authority from preserved historical comparisons
activation_signals: final_table_vs_gt_counts_by_doi.tsv records gt_authority_file with dev15_formulation_skeleton_review_v2_variantaware.xlsx
Run artifacts live under a gt_authority_v2_variantaware subdirectory or otherwise preserve the v2 authority path
regression_checks: Feature activation report evidence check on compare outputs
notes: This feature is active only when the run artifacts prove the v2 variant-aware workbook was actually used. Preserving the old GT authority file is part of the provenance contract.

title: Family and variant retention governance
problem_class: Stage5 final-output undercount or over-collapse when duplicate handling ignores family or variant identity
description: Controlled Stage5 governance layer that retains or collapses rows conservatively based on explicit family and variant evidence rather than broad ad hoc duplicate patches.
expected_intervention_points: Stage5 final-output build evaluates duplicate and variant governance rules
final_output_decision_trace_v1.tsv records phase1_variant_governance when the layer intervenes
final_formulation_table_v1.tsv preserves family and variant labels when rows survive
activation_signals: final_output_decision_trace_v1.tsv contains confidence_or_rule_scope=phase1_variant_governance
final_formulation_table_v1.tsv includes family_core or variant labels tied to retained rows
regression_checks: Stage5 replay reports under the current merged benchmark lineage
Feature activation report evidence check on phase1_variant_governance
notes: This feature can exist in the repo while still being inactive in a specific run. Activation must come from Stage5 run artifacts, not from the presence of the Stage5 code path alone.

title: Feature-unit governance layer
problem_class: Feature logic exists in code but is not explicitly registered, scoped, or checkable at run level
description: Machine-readable registry and intervention matrix that define benchmark-relevant feature units, their intended intervention points, and their regression checks.
expected_intervention_points: project/feature_units/feature_unit_registry.json exists and lists the run-relevant feature units
project/feature_units/feature_intervention_matrix.tsv exists and marks where each feature is required or optional
Run-level activation reporting consumes both files rather than inventing expectations ad hoc
activation_signals: feature_activation_report_v1.tsv exists for the run
The report was built from the registry and matrix under project/feature_units/
notes: UFXX9WXE showed that code-level fixes alone are not enough when a later benchmark run silently reuses older artifacts.

title: RUN_CONTEXT feature activation integration
problem_class: A run can look reproducible at the script IO level while still hiding missing feature interventions
description: Each benchmark-relevant run records its feature activation report path, required feature units, missing required feature units, activation gate, and compact activation table directly inside RUN_CONTEXT.md.
expected_intervention_points: RUN_CONTEXT.md includes a generated Feature Unit Activation section
The section references the run-local feature activation report
The run activation gate is derived from artifact-based feature statuses
activation_signals: RUN_CONTEXT.md contains the heading ## Feature Unit Activation
RUN_CONTEXT.md records run_activation_gate and feature_activation_report_path
notes: This makes feature integration status part of authoritative run metadata instead of a detached analysis-only artifact.

title: DOI-level GT versus prediction count audit
problem_class: Per-paper benchmark mismatches are hard to localize when only aggregate summaries are reported
description: Benchmark compare writes a DOI-level table with GT count, predicted final count, delta, and count status so run-level mismatches are auditable paper by paper.
expected_intervention_points: compare_final_table_to_gt_v1.py writes final_table_vs_gt_counts_by_doi.tsv
The run preserves the counts-by-doi artifact under the compare output root
RUN_CONTEXT.md or other run metadata can point to that compare artifact
activation_signals: final_table_vs_gt_counts_by_doi.tsv exists in the run
The TSV contains doi, gt_count, pred_count, delta_count, and count_status
notes: This is a benchmark-facing audit surface, not a substitute for the final formulation table itself.
